name: Aid
description:
When you help someone who has not yet rolled, the GM picks 1:

  • They can accomplish more than they could alone
  • They gain advantage on their roll

Either way, you are exposed to any risk, cost, or consequence associated with their roll.

name: Clash
description:
When you fight in melee or close quarters, roll +STR: on a 10+, your maneuver works as expected (deal your damage) and pick 1:

  • Avoid, prevent, or counter your enemy's attack
  • Strike hard and fast, for 1d6 extra damage, but suffer your enemy's attack

On a 7-9, your maneuver works, mostly (deal your damage), but you suffer your enemy's attack.

success.value:
Your maneuver works as expected (deal your damage) and pick 1:

  • Avoid, prevent, or counter your enemy's attack
  • Strike hard and fast, for 1d6 extra damage, but suffer your enemy's attack

partial.value: Your maneuver works, mostly (deal your damage), but you suffer your enemy's attack.

failure.value: Your maneuver fails and you suffer your enemy's attack. (Up to GM interpretation)

name: Defend
description:
When you take up a defensive stance or jump in to protect others, roll +CON: on a 10+, hold 3 Readiness (or 4 if you bear a shield); On a 7-9, hold 1 Readiness (or 2 with a shield). You can spend Readiness 1-for-1 to:

  • Suffer an attack's damage/effects instead of your ward
  • Halve an attack's effect or damage
  • Draw all attention from your ward to yourself
  • Strike back at an attacker (deal your damage, with disadvantage)

When you go on the offense, cease to focus on defense, or the threat passes, lose any Readiness that you hold.

success.value: Hold 3 Readiness (or 4 if you bear a shield).

failure.value: Escalate the situation aggressively, failure, etc.  (Up to GM interpretation).

name: Defy Danger
description:
When danger looms, the stakes are high, and you do something chancy, check if another move applies. If not, roll...

... +STR to power through or test your might

... +DEX to employ speed, agility, or finesse

... +CON to endure or hold steady

... +INT to apply expertise or enact a clever plan

... +WIS to exert willpower or rely on your senses

... +CHA to charm, bluff, impress, or fit in

On a 10+, you pull it off as well as one could hope; on a 7-9, you can do it, but the GM will present a lesser success, a cost, or a consequence (and maybe a choice between them, or a chance to back down).

success.value: You pull it off as well as one could hope.

partial.value: You can do it, but the GM will present a lesser success, a cost, or a consequence (and maybe a choice between them, or a chance to back down).

failure.value: Things get bad... things burn. (Up to GM interpretation)

name: Interfere
description:
When you try to foil another PC’s action and neither of you back down, roll...

... +STR to power through or test your might

... +DEX to employ speed, agility, or finesse

... +CON to endure or hold steady

... +INT to apply expertise or enact a clever plan

... +WIS to exert willpower or rely on your senses

... +CHA to charm, bluff, impress, or fit in

On a 10+, they pick 1 from the list below; on a 7-9, they pick 1 from the list below but you are left off-balance, exposed, or otherwise vulnerable.

  • Do it anyway, but with disadvantage on their (next) roll
  • Relent, change course, or otherwise allow their move to be foiled

partial.value: They pick 1 from the list but you are left off-balance, exposed, or otherwise vulnerable.

failure.value: Makes a hard GM move (Up to GM interpretation).

name: Know Things
description: When you consult your accumulated knowledge, roll +INT: on a 10+, The GM will tell you something interesting and useful about the topic at hand; On a 7-9, the GM will tell you something interesting—it's on you to make it useful;either way, the GM might ask “how do you know this?”

success.value: The GM will tell you something interesting and useful about the topic at hand.

partial.value: The GM will tell you something interesting—it's on you to make it useful.

failure.value: The GM will tell you something interesting, but it comes with a complication or cost. (Up to GM interpretation)

name: Let Fly
description:
When you calmly take an easy shot with a ranged weapon, deal your damage. If the shot is tricky or you’re under pressure, first roll +DEX: on a 10+, you have a clear shot, deal your damage; on a 7-9, pick 1:

  • Deal your damage, but deplete your ammo (mark the next status by your weapon; don’t pick this if your weapon lacks such statuses)
  • Hold steady and wait for a clear shot; when the moment arrives (GM’s call), deal your damage
  • Move to get a clear shot - exposing yourself to danger or giving up some advantage (GM says how) - then deal your damage
  • Rush the shot and deal your damage, leading to a cost or complication of the GM’s choice

success.value: You have a clear shot, deal your damage.

partial.value:
Pick 1:

  • Deal your damage, but deplete your ammo (mark the next status by your weapon; don’t pick this if your weapon lacks such statuses)
  • Hold steady and wait for a clear shot; when the moment arrives (GM’s call), deal your damage
  • Move to get a clear shot - exposing yourself to danger or giving up some advantage (GM says how) - then deal your damage
  • Rush the shot and deal your damage, leading to a cost or complication of the GM’s choice

name: Persuade (vs. NPCs)
description: When you press or entice an NPC, say what you want them to do (or not do). If they have reason to resist, roll +CHA: on a 10+, they either do as you want or reveal the easiest way to convince them; on a 7-9, they reveal something you can do to convince them, though it’ll likely be costly, tricky, or distasteful.

success.value: They either do as you want or reveal the easiest way to convince them

partial.value: They reveal something you can do to convince them, though it’ll likely be costly, tricky, or distasteful.

name: Persuade (vs. PCs)
description:
When you press or entice a PC and they resist, ask their player: “Could I possibly get you to do this, yes or no?” If they say “no,” let it drop.

If they say “yes,” you can roll +CHA: on a 10+, they mark XP if they do what you want, and if they don’t, they must say how you could convince them; on a 7-9, they mark XP if they do what you want (but can refuse or make a counter-offer if they like).

success.value: They mark XP if they do what you want, and if they don’t, they must say how you could convince them.

partial.value: They mark XP if they do what you want (but can refuse or make a counter-offer if they like).

name: Seek Insight
description:
When you study a situation or person, looking to the GM for insight, roll +WIS: on a 10+, ask the GM 3 questions from the list below; on a 7-9, ask 1; either way, gain advantage on your next move that acts on the answers.

  • What happened here?
  • What is about to happen?
  • What should I be on the lookout for?
  • What here is useful or valuable to me?
  • Who or what is really in control here?
  • What here is not what it appears to be?

success.value: Ask the GM 3 questions from the list. Gain advantage on your next move that acts on the answers.

partial.value: Ask the GM 1 question from the list. Gain advantage on your next move that acts on the answers.

failure.value: You miss something important or misread the situation. (Up to GM interpretation)
